---
title: 'Wisdom Loves You'
date: '2011-06-16T04:01:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3356339459357739844'
blogger_filename: '/2011/06/wisdom-loves-you.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'EMIC'
  - 'Mens Meeting'
---

Thoughts from the Mens Wed Morning Meeting 06/15<br /><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: collapse;">Have you ever noticed how childlike Benny Hinn is? He is always&nbsp;ecstatic&nbsp;about God's healing in people... He is always in awe about how much God loves him and his people... He is truly enveloped with the revelation of God's love for himself and for people... There is a sweetness to his anointing that is rare indeed. There are things about his personality that rub some people wrong, especially religious people... but if you place your&nbsp;preconceived&nbsp;ideas aside and spend some time getting to know him and his heart you can't help but love him. Nobody that loves God can help but love Benny Hinn and his unique relationship with God, even if you disagree with him on some point.&nbsp;</span></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><br /></span><br /><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;">Love is wisdom at it's highest point... Wisdom at it's highest level is understanding God's love. It's not just loving God, or loving people, or even loving your enemy. It's becoming totally absorbed with and saturated by God's love for you... and then God's love for people (Individually).&nbsp;</span><br /><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><br /></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;">That is the thing that has been burning in my heart since this mornings meeting. If you spend time becoming totally drowned in God's love for you it will be impossible for you not to have immediate access to the wisdom of God in each circumstance and for each person you are ministering to.&nbsp;</div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><br /></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;">Another example is Jesse Duplantis. "Hey Jesus!" "Hey Jesse!"... It's that closeness that allows both of these ministers to cross denominational lines. It's the love of God in and on Jesse that allows even Muslims to ask him to speak even though he's christian.&nbsp;</div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><br /></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;">Love/Wisdom two sides of the same coin. I've tried to love God and Folks... I've failed many times... It's time I start to build a foundation inside of me on the Love of God FOR me! All else follows that.&nbsp;</div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><br /></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><b><br /></b></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><b>James 3 is to Wisdom as 1 Corinthians 13 is to Love:</b></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><br /></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"></span><br /><div class="heading passage-class-0" style="color: #5c1101; margin-top: 10px;"><h3 style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: small;">James 3:13-18</span></h3><div class="txt-sm" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">New King James Version (NKJV)</span></div></div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span><br /><h5 class="passage-header"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">Heavenly Versus Demonic Wisdom</span></h5><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><i><br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-30329" style="font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">13</sup>&nbsp;Who&nbsp;is&nbsp;wise and understanding among you? Let him show by good conduct&nbsp;that&nbsp;his works&nbsp;are donein the meekness of wisdom.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-30330" style="font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">14</sup>&nbsp;But if you have bitter envy and self-seeking in your hearts, do not boast and lie against the truth.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-30331" style="font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">15</sup>&nbsp;This wisdom does not descend from above, but&nbsp;is&nbsp;earthly, sensual, demonic.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-30332" style="font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">16</sup>&nbsp;For where envy and self-seeking&nbsp;exist,&nbsp;confusion and every evil thing&nbsp;are&nbsp;there.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-30333" style="font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">17</sup>&nbsp;But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, willing to yield, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality and without hypocrisy.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-30334" style="font-weight: bold; line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">18</sup>&nbsp;Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.</i></span></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><br /></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"></span><br /><div class="heading passage-class-0" style="color: #5c1101; margin-top: 10px;"><h3 style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: small;">1 Corinthians 13</span></h3><div class="txt-sm" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">New King James Version (NKJV)</span></div><div class="txt-sm" style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><br /></span></div></div><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-weight: bold;">The Greatest Gift</span></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-weight: bold;"><br /></span></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><span class="Apple-style-span" style="border-collapse: separate; font-family: Verdana, Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;"><i>&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28663" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">1</sup>&nbsp;Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, but have not love, I have become sounding brass or a clanging cymbal.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28664" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">2</sup>&nbsp;And though I have&nbsp;the gift of&nbsp;prophecy, and understand all mysteries and all knowledge, and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, but have not love, I am nothing.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28665" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">3</sup>&nbsp;And though I bestow all my goods to feed&nbsp;the poor,&nbsp;and though I give my body to be burned,<sup class="footnote" style="line-height: 0.5em; vertical-align: text-top;" value="[&lt;a href=&quot;#fen-NKJV-28665a&quot; title=&quot;See footnote a&quot;&gt;a&lt;/a&gt;]">[<a href="http://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1%20Corinthians%2013&amp;version=NKJV#fen-NKJV-28665a" style="color: #651300; text-decoration: none;" title="See footnote a">a</a>]</sup>&nbsp;but have not love, it profits me nothing.<br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28666" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">4</sup>&nbsp;Love suffers long&nbsp;and&nbsp;is kind; love does not envy; love does not parade itself, is not puffed up;&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28667" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">5</sup>does not behave rudely, does not seek its own, is not provoked, thinks no evil;&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28668" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">6</sup>&nbsp;does not rejoice in iniquity, but rejoices in the truth;&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28669" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">7</sup>&nbsp;bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.<br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28670" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">8</sup>&nbsp;Love never fails. But whether&nbsp;there are&nbsp;prophecies, they will fail; whether&nbsp;there are&nbsp;tongues, they will cease; whether&nbsp;there is&nbsp;knowledge, it will vanish away.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28671" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">9</sup>&nbsp;For we know in part and we prophesy in part.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28672" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">10</sup>&nbsp;But when that which is perfect has come, then that which is in part will be done away.<br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28673" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">11</sup>&nbsp;When I was a child, I spoke as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child; but when I became a man, I put away childish things.&nbsp;<sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28674" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">12</sup>&nbsp;For now we see in a mirror, dimly, but then face to face. Now I know in part, but then I shall know just as I also am known.<br /><sup class="versenum" id="en-NKJV-28675" style="line-height: normal; vertical-align: text-top;">13</sup>&nbsp;And now abide faith, hope, love, these three; but the greatest of these&nbsp;is&nbsp;love.</i></span></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;"><br /></div><div style="border-collapse: collapse; font-family: arial, sans-serif;">Love, your brother in Christ, Darrell G. Wolfe</div>
